review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --disallowedTools "Read,Bash,Edit,Write,MultiEdit,Glob,Grep,LS" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-097-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: unauthorized_data_omitted is a constant; default visibility is fail-open
findings.description: executeCoreDomainApiContract always returns unauthorized_data_omitted:true regardless of whether anything was actually omitted. In serializeCoreDomainApiRecord, an empty allowed_record_ids returns every record, and an empty visible_fields falls back to DEFAULT_VISIBLE_FIELDS (tenant_id, display_name, status, etc.), so the default path is fail-open. Acceptable for RP01 synthetic-only (no real client data, tenant isolation still enforced via assertSameTenant), but the attestation does not reflect actual behavior.
findings.recommendation: Derive unauthorized_data_omitted from whether records/fields were actually dropped, and fail closed (empty projection / deny) when no allowlist is supplied, before any non-synthetic or LDIP wiring.

findings.title: Pagination total_count computed after slicing
findings.description: createCoreDomainApiPagination is invoked with filtered.length, where filtered is already .slice(0, limit). total_count can therefore never exceed limit, so next_cursor is always null and returned_count always equals total_count, making pagination metadata inaccurate when records exceed page size. Not a boundary violation; synthetic-only.
findings.recommendation: Pass the pre-slice record count as total_count and slice separately for returned data.

findings.title: DEFAULT_VISIBLE_FIELDS lists matter_id twice
findings.description: const DEFAULT_VISIBLE_FIELDS includes 'matter_id' at two positions. Harmless (serialization is idempotent) but indicates a copy mistake.

findings.title: workflow.execute branch returns full result without visibility serialization
findings.description: The core_domain.workflow.execute branch returns the entire workflow object in data[] and still asserts unauthorized_data_omitted:true without running any omission/allowlist logic for that path. Synthetic-only and no-write, so no data-exposure impact in RP01.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same visibility projection (or explicitly document that workflow.execute is exempt) so the omission attestation is consistent across endpoints.

notes: No-write boundaries satisfied: no audit writes, no product/database writes, no runtime permission evaluation; all attestation flags false in api.js, golden-cases.js, and contract JSON.
Synthetic-only enforced: createCoreDomainApiRequest throws on synthetic_only===false; fixtures used by tests/validator.
Tenant isolation enforced (assertSameTenant over context.records, fail-closed via catch->status:blocked).
HRX remains embedded and ldip_implemented:false in closeout handoff; review packet scope does not implement LDIP.
Contract/validator/test alignment is consistent: current_pack->CP00-097 (Risk C, 150 units), prior CP00-096 moved to workflow_pack, schema_version v0.4, ranges match bindings.
All findings are non-blocking P3 correctness/hardening nits; no P0/P1/P2, so pack and goal closeout are not blocked.
